chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Survey of Optimization. (3-0). Credit 3. Theory and numerical methods for deterministic linear and nonlinear optimization; topics include linear programming, unconstrained-nonlinear optimization, constrained-nonlinear optimization, Lagrange and K-K-T conditions, and numerical algorithms. Prerequisite: MATH 304 or MATH 311.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Grade Decomposition

| Tasks | Percentage |
| :---------------------- | :--------- |
| Homework Assignments | 10% |
| Midterm Exam | 40% |
| Final Exam | 50% |

Both exams are closed-book. The tentative date for the midterm is February 24 (in-class). The final exam is scheduled on May 5, 1-3 pm. A 1-page cheat sheet and a calculator (with no Internet access) are allowed for both exams.

Homework assignments will be given biweekly. Canvas is set up to allow multiple submissions and only the last submitted assignment will be graded. Late submission will incur a deduction of 5% per hour. Some homework assignments will ask students to use computer tools. For these assignments, students need to submit their source code, together with the computer outputs. The source code must be done using designated computer programming languages, or those approved by the instructor.

Final grades will be determined as follows.

Letter Grade Cutoffs

| Range | Letter Grade |
| :---------- | :----------- |
| [90%, 100%] | A |
| [80%, 90%) | B |
| [70%, 80%) | C |
| [60%, 70%) | D |
| [0%, 60%) | F |

About regrading, assignment or exam regrading requests will be considered if received in writing via email, with the original work attached to your instructor or the grader **no later than one week after the material was returned**. Note that the instructor and the grader have the right to regrade the entirety, so it is possible to lose points. Therefore, it is recommended that students should ask for regrading only there is substantial reason that the grade is given in error.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Late submission for homework assignments is accepted with the reduction in points (5% per hour). Makeup exams are only available for excused absence defined in Student Rule 7.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
The following course schedule is tentative and subject to change, depending on the actual progress and student feedback.

• Week 1-4: Introduction and linear optimization modeling
• Week 5-8: Simplex methods, linear optimization duality, and sensitivity analysis
• Week 9-10: Introduction to integer and mixed-integer optimization methods
• Week 11-15: Selected topics in nonlinear optimization
